message:
test(wasm): cover RunnerCore pane_context_lost + pane_render_stalled

Follow-up to 686d4560 (feat(wasm): expose paneContextLost +
paneRenderStalled bindings). That commit added the wasm surface
that forwards to the RunnerCore hooks added in a2e4c5dd; this
commit locks the RunnerCore-level semantics with two regression
tests mirroring the adapter-level tests already in ftui-web:

1. runner_core_pane_context_lost_releases_active_capture
   - pointer_down on a test target with pointer id 97, then
     capture_acquired(97) to put the machine in the
     captured-drag state.
   - Call pane_context_lost() and assert the returned
     PaneDispatchSummary is accepted, the outcome is
     SemanticForwarded (the runner emitted a real semantic
     event, not just a local log), the capture_command is
     Some(Release { pointer_id: 97 }) so the browser will drop
     the capture, and pane_active_pointer_id() reads None.

2. runner_core_pane_render_stalled_before_capture_ack_cancels_without_release
   - pointer_down(98) but NO capture_acquired — we're in the
     capture-pending window.
   - Call pane_render_stalled() and assert the dispatch is
     accepted, outcome is SemanticForwarded, capture_command is
     None (no capture to release yet — issuing a spurious
     Release on a pointer we never captured would trip the
     browser's "release on un-captured pointer" warning), and
     pane_active_pointer_id() reads None.

Both tests mirror the earlier adapter-level coverage in
crates/ftui-web/src/pane_pointer_capture.rs so the same
invariants now hold at two layers: the raw adapter that owns
the state machine, and the RunnerCore glue that threads
dispatches back to the host.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
